title: festuca orthophylla subvar. boliviana
intro: Festuca orthophylla subvar. boliviana, also known as Bolivian fescue, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Bolivian fescue include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, decocting the roots to create a medicinal infusion, or using the plant's essential oils in aromatherapy preparations.
